settings.title: Settings
settings.defaultPositionHint: Where the overlay anchors on screen
settings.shareLinux: Experimental on Linux — depends on your compositor
settings.trackingModeHint: How the prompter follows your pace
settings.keepMicOpenHint: Avoids the system listening chime each time you resume
settings.textSizeHint: Default font size in the overlay
settings.glassBlurHint: Backdrop blur intensity behind the panel
settings.timerHint: Timer mode shown in the overlay
settings.restoreDefaultsHint: Reset all reading defaults to built-in values
settings.reduceMotionHint: Disables easing and smooth-scroll animations in the overlay
settings.highContrastHint: Stronger text shadow for legibility over complex backgrounds
settings.hkInteractHint: Lets clicks pass through the glass — works system-wide
settings.micAccessHint: Needed for voice-tracking mode; speech recognition access is requested alongside it
settings.speechAccessHint: Needed to turn your voice into text for the prompter to follow
settings.dictationAccessHint: A separate OS setting the app can't detect or turn on for you — voice tracking needs it even once mic and speech recognition above are granted
settings.voicePermissionsHint: Microphone, speech recognition, and Dictation — everything voice tracking needs
settings.updateError: Couldn't check for updates — try again later
settings.updateCheckFrequencyHint: How often to check for updates in the background
settings.donateHint: If it helps you, help keep it maintained — funded by people, not paywalls.
settings.dyslexicFontHint: OpenDyslexic font with roomier spacing in the prompter

overlay.dragHint: Drag to move · Esc to hide
overlay.enableMicHint: Voice tracking needs one click inside the overlay to start the mic
overlay.micIssueHint: Check System Settings → Keyboard → Dictation is on — it's separate from mic permission and required for voice tracking. Resumes automatically once fixed.
overlay.demoTitle: We shipped invisible. Now we scale it.
overlay.demoSub: Three numbers that defined the quarter — and where the next one takes us.

shield.hiddenTitle: Hidden from screen share — click to expose
shield.visibleTitle: Visible in screen share — click to hide

consent.title: Screen-share hiding is experimental on Linux
consent.introA: On macOS and Windows the operating system guarantees this window is excluded from screen capture.
consent.li0a: Whether the overlay is hidden depends entirely on your compositor (KWin, Mutter, wlroots, X11…).
consent.li0b: On most setups it will not be hidden.
consent.li1: Your script may be fully visible to anyone you are sharing with, or appear in recordings.
consent.li2: Always verify with a test recording before relying on it in a real call.
consent.question: Do you understand the risk and want to enable it anyway? You won’t be asked again.

permissions.title: Set up voice tracking
permissions.intro: Voice tracking follows along as you read by listening through your mic. It needs a couple of quick permissions first.
